mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and economic activity, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 55% of periods when RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by over 0.5pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high inflation and rising interest rates indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle by the Federal Reserve, which aims to cool the economy, leading to higher discount rates and lower equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 3% AND FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% in 1 month
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both CPIAUCSL is above 3% and FEDFUNDS has increased by over 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production during an economic expansion signifies healthy manufacturing activity and business investment, which underpins corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: During periods of confirmed economic expansion, INDPRO growth does not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with S&P 500 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Robust quarterly GDP growth reflects a strong and expanding overall economy, which typically translates into higher corporate profits, increased investor confidence, and ultimately drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 30% of quarters following GDPC1 annualized growth exceeding 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job creation, as indicated by non-farm payrolls, signals a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS increases by over 100,000 persons month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of weakening future construction activity and broader economic demand, often preceding a general economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods following a month-over-month decline in HOUST exceeding 10%.

mechanism_narrative: A reduction in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy stance, which lowers borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, stimulating economic activity and supporting higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 30% of periods following a FEDFUNDS rate cut of over 0.25%.
